taxonomy.family_description: Nightshade family; tomato, potato, pepper, tobacco

th.local_name: Makhua thet (มะเขือเทศ)

climate.temp_optimum: Optimal 20-24°C day; 13-17°C night; no frost tolerance
climate.rainfall: 600-1500mm; avoid during fruiting; ideal with irrigation

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam with good organic matter

irrigation.method: Drip irrigation preferred; avoid overhead (disease); furrow if drip unavailable

fertilization.schedule: Split N and K through fertigation (8-12 splits through season)

symptoms.early: Light brown water-soaked area at blossom end of green fruit
symptoms.late: Expanding dark brown to black leathery sunken patch at fruit base, secondary fungal colonization

treatment.organic: Foliar calcium chloride spray (4g/L), consistent irrigation, mulching, agricultural lime in soil
treatment.chemical: Calcium nitrate foliar spray (2g/L), drip application of calcium chelate
treatment.preventive: Consistent irrigation schedule, mulch to retain soil moisture, soil pH 6.2-6.8, avoid excess ammonium nitrogen

symptoms.early: Wilting during hot hours, poor plant vigor, chlorosis resembling nutrient deficiency
symptoms.late: Typical galls (root knots) on roots when pulled, stunted growth, reduced yield

treatment.organic: Marigold intercropping (Tagetes sp.), neem cake soil application, Paecilomyces lilacinus, Pochonia chlamydosporia
treatment.chemical: Carbofuran 3G (33kg/ha soil application), Ethoprophos 10G granules, Oxamyl
treatment.biological: Purpureocillium lilacinum, Trichoderma harzianum, Bacillus firmus
treatment.preventive: Crop rotation with non-host crops (maize, cereals), soil solarization, use resistant varieties (Mi-gene)

symptoms.early: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves starting on one side of the plant, often one branch first
symptoms.late: Complete wilting and death, brown internal vascular discoloration visible when stem is cut crosswise

treatment.organic: Trichoderma harzianum soil treatment, Solarization (clear polyethylene film over moist soil for 4-6 weeks)
treatment.chemical: No effective chemical cure; soil fumigation with Metam sodium before planting as preventive
treatment.biological: Trichoderma viride, Pseudomonas fluorescens root dip, Bacillus subtilis
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (race-specific), crop rotation (4+ years), avoid wounding roots, soil solarization

symptoms.early: Water-soaked irregular grayish-green patches on leaves, usually starting from leaf tips and edges
symptoms.late: Fuzzy white sporulating lesions on underside of leaves in humid conditions, greasy dark green-black patches on fruit, entire plant collapse with putrid smell

treatment.organic: Copper hydroxide (3g/L) as protective spray, Bordeaux mixture (1%), maintain dry conditions
treatment.chemical: Metalaxyl-M + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) 2.5g/L, Cymoxanil + Famoxadone 0.5g/L, Dimethomorph 0.5g/L
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, ensure good drainage, avoid overhead irrigation, scout regularly during cool wet weather, destroy volunteer plants

symptoms.early: Small, circular water-soaked spots on lower leaves with dark brown margins and lighter gray-white centers with tiny black dots (pycnidia)
symptoms.late: Extensive defoliation starting from bottom of plant upward, sun-scalded fruits exposed to direct sun

treatment.organic: Copper oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L), neem oil spray, remove infected lower leaves
treatment.preventive: Crop rotation, remove infected plant debris, avoid overhead irrigation, stake and prune for air circulation

symptoms.early: Upward and inward curling of leaflets, yellowing at leaf margins
symptoms.late: Severe stunting, complete yellowing, flower drop, no fruit set or deformed fruits

treatment.organic: No cure once infected; control whitefly vector with neem oil, yellow sticky traps, reflective mulch
treatment.chemical: Imidacloprid or Thiamethoxam for whitefly control. Mineral oil sprays to reduce whitefly transmission
treatment.biological: Encarsia formosa parasitoid, Beauveria bassiana for whiteflies
treatment.preventive: Plant TYLCV-resistant varieties, use insect-proof netting in nursery, roguing infected plants immediately, control whiteflies

symptoms.early: Pale yellow spots on upper leaf surface, white powdery growth visible on underside
symptoms.late: Entire leaf covered with white powder, premature defoliation

treatment.organic: Wettable sulfur (3g/L), potassium bicarbonate (5g/L), neem oil, diluted milk (1:9)
treatment.preventive: Good air circulation, avoid dense planting, resistant varieties

symptoms.early: Sudden wilting of top leaves during hottest part of the day, recovery at night
symptoms.late: Permanent wilting even at night, water-soaked stem interior, oozing of bacterial slime (white to gray) from cut stems dipped in water

treatment.organic: No effective cure. Copper-based bactericides as weak suppressants
treatment.biological: Bacillus subtilis and Pseudomonas fluorescens as biological suppressants (preventive only)
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties, crop rotation (minimum 3 years), soil solarization, avoid waterlogging, remove infected plants and soil, use clean tools

symptoms.early: Small, dark brown circular spots with concentric rings (target board pattern) on older lower leaves
symptoms.late: Leaf yellowing, premature leaf drop, dark sunken lesions at stem-ends of fruit, stem collar rot

treatment.organic: Neem oil (4ml/L + 2ml soap), Copper oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L), Trichoderma harzianum soil drench, Baking soda spray (5g/L)
treatment.chemical: Mancozeb 75% WP (2g/L), Azoxystrobin 23% SC (1ml/L), Chlorothalonil 75% WP (2g/L), Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L)
treatment.biological: Trichoderma viride, Bacillus subtilis strains QST713, Pseudomonas fluorescens
treatment.preventive: Use certified disease-free seeds, crop rotation (3 years), remove infected debris, avoid overhead irrigation, stake plants for air circulation

control.organic: Yellow sticky traps, neem oil + soap spray (5ml+2ml per L), reflective mulch, soap water spray, Beauveria bassiana
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L), Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.2g/L), Acetamiprid 20% SP (0.2g/L), Spiromesifen (resistance management)
control.biological: Encarsia formosa (parasitic wasp), Eretmocerus mundus, Macrolophus pygmaeus (predatory bug), Beauveria bassiana

pests.damage_type: Leaf and whorl feeding; ear damage

control.organic: Bt-kurstaki or Bt-aizawai spray; fall armyworm pheromone traps; sand+soil in whorl (suffocates larvae); egg mass and larvae hand collection
control.chemical: Emamectin benzoate 5% SG (0.4g/L into whorl), Chlorantraniliprole (Coragen), Spinetoram, Lambda-cyhalothrin (adult moths)
control.biological: Telenomus remus (egg parasitoid), Cotesia icipe, Coccygomimus turionellae, Metarhizium rileyi, NPV (Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus)

control.organic: NPV spray (2×10¹² POB/ha); HaNPV + cotton Bt; pheromone traps (1/ha); neem seed kernel extract; Bt-k spray
control.chemical: Emamectin benzoate 5% SG (0.4g/L), Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC (0.3ml/L), Indoxacarb, Flubendiamide, Thiodicarb
control.biological: Trichogramma chilonis (egg parasitoid, 1 lakh/ha weekly), Habrobracon hebetor, Chrysoperla carnea, HaNPV

taxonomy.family_description: Nightshade family; tomato, potato, pepper, tobacco

th.local_name: Man farang (มันฝรั่ง)

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam or loam; deep friable soils
soil.special_requirements: Deep tillage for loose soil for tuber expansion

irrigation.critical_stages: Planting, tuber initiation (60-70 DAS), tuber bulking

fertilization.schedule: All at planting or split with K

harvesting.maturity_indicator: Vine death; skin doesn't peel off when rubbed
harvesting.method: Mechanical digger or manual fork; avoid damage

symptoms.early: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaves, white sporulation on underside in humid conditions
symptoms.late: Dark brown-black lesions on leaves, brown rots on stems, rapid spread of entire field collapse, tuber rot (brown-red internal)

treatment.organic: Copper hydroxide (2.5-3g/L) preventively, Bordeaux mixture, avoid excessive irrigation
treatment.chemical: Metalaxyl-M + Mancozeb 2.5g/L, Dimethomorph + Mancozeb, Cymoxanil + Famoxadone, Fluopicolide
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Bahar, Jyoti), use certified disease-free seed tubers, apply fungicide before first rains, destroy volunteer plants

symptoms.early: No visible early symptoms on foliage
symptoms.late: Brown corky scab lesions (raised, sunken, or russeted) on tuber surface; external quality defect only

treatment.organic: Soil acidification (sulfur), crop rotation, adequate irrigation during tuber formation
treatment.chemical: Quintozene PCNB seed treatment (limited), Flutolanil seed treatment
treatment.biological: Fluorescent Pseudomonas, Bacillus subtilis soil inoculants
treatment.preventive: Lower soil pH to 5.0-5.5, maintain soil moisture during tuber initiation, rotate with non-susceptible crops (cereals), use scab-resistant varieties

control.organic: Yellow sticky traps, neem oil + soap spray (5ml+2ml per L), reflective mulch, soap water spray, Beauveria bassiana
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L), Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.2g/L), Acetamiprid 20% SP (0.2g/L), Spiromesifen (resistance management)
control.biological: Encarsia formosa (parasitic wasp), Eretmocerus mundus, Macrolophus pygmaeus (predatory bug), Beauveria bassiana

control.organic: Metarhizium anisopliae fungus (1×10⁹ spores/ml) soil application; ploughing to expose and destroy grubs; light traps for adults; neem cake 250 kg/ha
control.chemical: Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (5L/ha) soil incorporation before planting; Carbofuran 3G (25 kg/ha); Phorate 10G; Imidacloprid seed treatment
control.biological: Metarhizium anisopliae, Beauveria bassiana soil inoculants; Heterorhabditis bacteriophora (entomopathogenic nematode)

pests.scientific_name: Amrasca biguttula biguttula (Cotton/Okra Leafhopper); Empoasca spp.

control.organic: Neem seed kernel extract 5%, neem oil; yellow sticky traps; Beauveria bassiana spray
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.3ml/L), Lambda-cyhalothrin 5% EC (1ml/L), Thiamethoxam, Dimethoate 30% EC (2ml/L)
control.biological: Anagrus atomus (egg parasitoid in grapes), Gonatocerus ashmeadi, Stethorus ladybugs

symptoms.early: Water-soaked, brown lesions on petals, leaves, and stems; soft rot
symptoms.late: Characteristic gray fluffy fungal sporulation covering lesions; blight of flowers, stem cankers, fruit rot ("ghost spot" on tomato)
symptoms.affected_parts: Flowers, Leaves, Stems, Fruit, Storage organs

treatment.organic: Remove infected tissue, improve air circulation, reduce humidity; Bacillus subtilis, Trichoderma spray
treatment.chemical: Iprodione (1.5g/L), Pyrimethanil (1ml/L), Fenhexamid (1g/L), Cyprodinil (0.5g/L). Rotate fungicides for resistance management.
treatment.biological: Bacillus subtilis QST713 (Serenade), Trichoderma asperellum, Clonostachys rosea (Prestop)
treatment.preventive: Improve air circulation, avoid wounding, remove dead tissue, avoid waterlogging, maintain low humidity

pests.scientific_name: Thrips palmi (Melon Thrips); Frankliniella occidentalis (Western Flower Thrips)

control.organic: Blue sticky traps (most attractive to thrips), spinosad spray, neem oil, kaolin clay, predatory mites (Neoseiulus cucumeris)
control.chemical: Spinosad 45% SC (0.3ml/L), Imidacloprid, Abamectin 1.9% EC (0.5ml/L), Cyantraniliprole, Tolfenpyrad
control.biological: Neoseiulus cucumeris, Amblyseius swirskii (predatory mites); Orius insidiosus (pirate bug); Steinernema feltiae (entomopathogenic nematode) for soil pupae

symptoms.early: Water-soaked lesions on lower stems and branches; bleached, straw-colored stem tissue
symptoms.late: White cottony mycelial growth inside and outside infected tissue, large hard black sclerotia (2-10mm) inside stem

treatment.organic: Thiram-treated seeds, adequate plant spacing, crop rotation
treatment.chemical: Thiophanate-methyl (1g/L), Boscalid (0.5g/L), Iprodione, Fluazinam at early flowering
treatment.biological: Coniothyrium minitans (Contans WG) — applied to soil to parasitize sclerotia
treatment.preventive: Crop rotation (non-host crops for 3+ years), avoid dense planting, improve drainage

control.organic: Bacillus thuringiensis subsp. kurstaki (Bt-k) spray (most effective); neem seed kernel extract 5%; hand picking; pheromone traps for monitoring
control.chemical: Spinosad 45% SC (0.3ml/L), Indoxacarb 14.5% SC (1ml/L), Chlorfluazuron (IGR), Emamectin benzoate 5% SG (0.2g/L), Flubendiamide
control.biological: Cotesia plutellae (parasitic wasp), Diadegma semiclausum, Diadromus collaris; Bacillus thuringiensis

symptoms.early: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaves, white sporulation on underside in humid conditions
symptoms.late: Dark brown-black lesions on leaves, brown rots on stems, rapid spread of entire field collapse, tuber rot (brown-red internal)

treatment.organic: Copper hydroxide (2.5-3g/L) preventively, Bordeaux mixture, avoid excessive irrigation
treatment.chemical: Metalaxyl-M + Mancozeb 2.5g/L, Dimethomorph + Mancozeb, Cymoxanil + Famoxadone, Fluopicolide
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Bahar, Jyoti), use certified disease-free seed tubers, apply fungicide before first rains, destroy volunteer plants

symptoms.early: No visible early symptoms on foliage
symptoms.late: Brown corky scab lesions (raised, sunken, or russeted) on tuber surface; external quality defect only

treatment.organic: Soil acidification (sulfur), crop rotation, adequate irrigation during tuber formation
treatment.chemical: Quintozene PCNB seed treatment (limited), Flutolanil seed treatment
treatment.biological: Fluorescent Pseudomonas, Bacillus subtilis soil inoculants
treatment.preventive: Lower soil pH to 5.0-5.5, maintain soil moisture during tuber initiation, rotate with non-susceptible crops (cereals), use scab-resistant varieties

control.organic: Yellow sticky traps, neem oil + soap spray (5ml+2ml per L), reflective mulch, soap water spray, Beauveria bassiana
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L), Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.2g/L), Acetamiprid 20% SP (0.2g/L), Spiromesifen (resistance management)
control.biological: Encarsia formosa (parasitic wasp), Eretmocerus mundus, Macrolophus pygmaeus (predatory bug), Beauveria bassiana

control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L), Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.2g/L), Acetamiprid 20% SP (0.2g/L), Spiromesifen (resistance management)

Daisy/Composite family; world's largest flowering plant family

symptoms.late: Characteristic gray fluffy fungal sporulation covering lesions; blight of flowers, stem cankers, fruit rot ("ghost spot" on tomato)
symptoms.affected_parts: Flowers, Leaves, Stems, Fruit, Storage organs

control.biological: Cotesia plutellae (parasitic wasp), Diadegma semiclausum, Diadromus collaris; Bacillus thuringiensis
